title: Induction of aggregation in alpha-synuclein-expressing cells by treatment with preformed fibrils (PFFs)

steps: Seed cells into plates such that they are ~25% confluent the following day. For our HEK cells, the correct seeding density is ~250 cells/mm 2 , which is equivalent to 100,000 cells in a 12-well dish.

On the following day, warm PBS and OptiMEM to 37 °C , thaw an aliquot of PFFs, and (if using) allow lipofectamine to reach Room temperature . Lipofectamine greatly increases the ability of PFFs to nucleate intracellular alpha synuclein aggregation, perhaps by altering the endocytic route that PFFs use to enter the cell. It is therefore not recommended to use lipofectamine if the route of PFF entry is a concern in your experiment. Lipofectamine is additionally excessively toxic and should therefore be avoided in some cell types.
Dilute the thawed PFFs 1:20 into an eppendorf containing PBS. The final volume must be between 100 µL and 300 µL for proper sonication in the Bioruptor Plus.
Sonicate the PFFs in the Bioruptor Plus on high for 25 cycles of 00:00:05 on and 00:00:05 off at 4 °C . Sonication breaks up the PFFs into smaller, more nucleation-competent fibrils.
Make a master mix of PFF and PBS solutions.
1 µL of sonicated & diluted PFFs should be added per 10 mm 2 of plate area (5 ug alpha-synuclein/10 mm 2 ). For a 12-well plate, this would be 40 µL of sonicated & diluted PFFs.

Master mixes should contain a ratio of 50 µL OptiMEM : 20 µL sonicated & diluted PFFs (or PBS as a control) : 3 µL of lipofectamine 3000. For a 12-well plate, 100 OptiMEM : 40 µL sonicated & diluted PFFs/PBS : 6 µL lipofectamine 300 should be used.
If using lipofectamine, first incubate the lipofectamine 3000 in OptiMEM for 00:05:00 before adding PFFs or PBS. Gently vortex upon addition of lipofectamine 3000.
After addition of PFFs/PBS, gently vortex master mix solutions and incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature .
Add master mix solutions dropwise to cells, gently vortexing before adding to each well.
creator: Cole Sitron
